name: pretty-midi

metadata.name: pretty_midi
metadata.summary: Functions and classes for handling MIDI data conveniently.

documentation:
Functions and classes which make handling MIDI data easy in Python.
    Provides methods for parsing, modifying, and analyzing MIDI files.

modules.name: pretty_midi.constants
documentation:
This file defines MIDI standard constants, which are useful for converting
between numeric MIDI data values and human-readable text.

modules.name: pretty_midi
documentation:
`pretty_midi` contains utility function/classes for handling MIDI data,
so that it's in a format from which it is easy to modify and extract
information.
If you end up using `pretty_midi` in a published research project, please
cite the following report:

Colin Raffel and Daniel P. W. Ellis.
[Intuitive Analysis, Creation and Manipulation of MIDI Data with pretty_midi](http://colinraffel.com/publications/ismir2014intuitive.pdf).
In 15th International Conference on Music Information Retrieval Late Breaking
and Demo Papers, 2014.

Example usage for analyzing, manipulating and synthesizing a MIDI file:

```python
import pretty_midi
# Load MIDI file into PrettyMIDI object
midi_data = pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI('example.mid')
# Print an empirical estimate of its global tempo
print midi_data.estimate_tempo()
# Compute the relative amount of each semitone across the entire song,
# a proxy for key
total_velocity = sum(sum(midi_data.get_chroma()))
print [sum(semitone)/total_velocity for semitone in midi_data.get_chroma()]
# Shift all notes up by 5 semitones
for instrument in midi_data.instruments:
    # Don't want to shift drum notes
    if not instrument.is_drum:
        for note in instrument.notes:
            note.pitch += 5
# Synthesize the resulting MIDI data using sine waves
audio_data = midi_data.synthesize()
```

Example usage for creating a simple MIDI file:

```python
import pretty_midi
# Create a PrettyMIDI object
cello_c_chord = pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI()
# Create an Instrument instance for a cello instrument
cello_program = pretty_midi.instrument_name_to_program('Cello')
cello = pretty_midi.Instrument(program=cello_program)
# Iterate over note names, which will be converted to note number later
for note_name in ['C5', 'E5', 'G5']:
    # Retrieve the MIDI note number for this note name
    note_number = pretty_midi.note_name_to_number(note_name)
    # Create a Note instance, starting at 0s and ending at .5s
    note = pretty_midi.Note(
        velocity=100, pitch=note_number, start=0, end=.5)
    # Add it to our cello instrument
    cello.notes.append(note)
# Add the cello instrument to the PrettyMIDI object
cello_c_chord.instruments.append(cello)
# Write out the MIDI data
cello_c_chord.write('cello-C-chord.mid')
```

Further examples can be found in the source tree's [examples directory](https://github.com/craffel/pretty-midi/tree/master/examples).

modules.name: pretty_midi.containers
documentation: These classes simply hold MIDI data in a convenient form.

classes.name: pretty_midi.containers.Note

methods.name: pretty_midi.containers.Note.get_duration
documentation: Get the duration of the note in seconds.

A note event.

Parameters
----------
velocity : int
    Note velocity.
pitch : int
    Note pitch, as a MIDI note number.
start : float
    Note on time, absolute, in seconds.
end : float
    Note off time, absolute, in seconds.

classes.name: pretty_midi.containers.PitchBend
documentation:
A pitch bend event.

# Parameters

pitch

: MIDI pitch bend amount, in the range `[-8192, 8191]`.

time

: Time where the pitch bend occurs.

classes.name: pretty_midi.containers.ControlChange
documentation:
A control change event.

# Parameters

number

: The control change number, in `[0, 127]`.

value

: The value of the control change, in `[0, 127]`.

time

: Time where the control change occurs.

classes.name: pretty_midi.containers.TimeSignature
documentation:
Container for a Time Signature event, which contains the time signature
numerator, denominator and the event time in seconds.

# Attributes

numerator

: Numerator of time signature.

denominator

: Denominator of time signature.

time

: Time of event in seconds.

# Examples

Instantiate a TimeSignature object with 6/8 time signature at 3.14 seconds:

```pycon
>>> ts = TimeSignature(6, 8, 3.14)
>>> print(ts)
6/8 at 3.14 seconds
```

classes.name: pretty_midi.containers.KeySignature
documentation:
Contains the key signature and the event time in seconds.
Only supports major and minor keys.

# Attributes

key_number

: Key number according to `[0, 11]` Major, `[12, 23]` minor.
  For example, 0 is C Major, 12 is C minor.

time

: Time of event in seconds.

# Examples

Instantiate a C# minor KeySignature object at 3.14 seconds:

```pycon
>>> ks = KeySignature(13, 3.14)
>>> print(ks)
C# minor at 3.14 seconds
```

classes.name: pretty_midi.containers.Lyric
documentation:
Timestamped lyric text.

Attributes
----------
text : str
    The text of the lyric.
time : float
    The time in seconds of the lyric.

classes.name: pretty_midi.containers.Text
documentation:
Timestamped text event.

Attributes
----------
text : str
    The text.
time : float
    The time it occurs in seconds.

modules.name: pretty_midi.utilities
documentation:
Utilty functions for converting between MIDI data and human-readable/usable
values

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.key_number_to_key_name
documentation:
Convert a key number to a key string.

# Parameters

key_number

: Uses pitch classes to represent major and minor keys.
  For minor keys, adds a 12 offset.
  For example, C major is 0 and C minor is 12.

# Returns

key_name

: Key name in the format `'(root) (mode)'`, e.g. `'Gb minor'`.
  Gives preference for keys with flats, with the exception of F#, G# and
  C# minor.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.key_name_to_key_number
documentation:
Convert a key name string to key number.

# Parameters

key_string
: Format is `'(root) (mode)'`, where:
  : - `(root)` is one of ABCDEFG or abcdefg. A lowercase root
      indicates a minor key when no mode string is specified. Optionally
      a # for sharp or b for flat can be specified.
    - `(mode)` is optionally specified either as one of 'M', 'Maj',
      'Major', 'maj', or 'major' for major or 'm', 'Min', 'Minor', 'min',
      'minor' for minor. If no mode is specified and the root is
      uppercase, the mode is assumed to be major; if the root is
      lowercase, the mode is assumed to be minor.

# Returns

key_number

: Integer representing the key and its mode. Integers from 0 to 11
  represent major keys from C to B; 12 to 23 represent minor keys from C
  to B.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.mode_accidentals_to_key_number
documentation:
Convert a given number of accidentals and mode to a key number.

Parameters
----------
mode : int
    0 is major, 1 is minor.
num_accidentals : int
    Positive number is used for sharps, negative number is used for flats.

Returns
-------
key_number : int
    Integer representing the key and its mode.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.key_number_to_mode_accidentals
documentation:
Converts a key number to number of accidentals and mode.

# Parameters

key_number

: Key number as used in `pretty_midi`.

# Returns

mode

: 0 for major, 1 for minor.

num_accidentals

: Number of accidentals.
  Positive is for sharps and negative is for flats.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.qpm_to_bpm
documentation:
Converts from quarter notes per minute to beats per minute.

Parameters
----------
quarter_note_tempo : float
    Quarter note tempo.
numerator : int
    Numerator of time signature.
denominator : int
    Denominator of time signature.

Returns
-------
bpm : float
    Tempo in beats per minute.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.note_number_to_hz
documentation:
Convert a (fractional) MIDI note number to its frequency in Hz.

Parameters
----------
note_number : float
    MIDI note number, can be fractional.

Returns
-------
note_frequency : float
    Frequency of the note in Hz.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.hz_to_note_number
documentation:
Convert a frequency in Hz to a (fractional) note number.

Parameters
----------
frequency : float
    Frequency of the note in Hz.

Returns
-------
note_number : float
    MIDI note number, can be fractional.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.note_name_to_number
documentation:
Converts a note name in the format
`'(note)(accidental)(octave number)'` (e.g. `'C#4'`) to MIDI note
number.

`'(note)'` is required, and is case-insensitive.

`'(accidental)'` should be `''` for natural, `'#'` for sharp and
`'!'` or `'b'` for flat.

If `'(octave)'` is `''`, octave 0 is assumed.

# Parameters

note_name

: A note name, as described above.

# Returns

note_number

: MIDI note number corresponding to the provided note name.

# Notes

> Thanks to Brian McFee.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.note_number_to_name
documentation:
Convert a MIDI note number to its name, in the format
`'(note)(accidental)(octave number)'` (e.g. `'C#4'`).

# Parameters

note_number

: MIDI note number. If not an int, it will be rounded.

# Returns

note_name

: Name of the supplied MIDI note number.

# Notes

> Thanks to Brian McFee.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.note_number_to_drum_name
documentation:
Converts a MIDI note number in a percussion instrument to the
corresponding drum name, according to the General MIDI standard.

Any MIDI note number outside of the valid range (note 35-81, zero-indexed)
will result in an empty string.

Parameters
----------
note_number : int
    MIDI note number.  If not an int, it will be rounded.

Returns
-------
drum_name : str
    Name of the drum for this note for a percussion instrument.

Notes
-----
    See http://www.midi.org/techspecs/gm1sound.php

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.__normalize_str
documentation:
Removes all non-alphanumeric characters from a string and converts
it to lowercase.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.drum_name_to_note_number
documentation:
Converts a drum name to the corresponding MIDI note number for a
percussion instrument.  Conversion is case, whitespace, and
non-alphanumeric character insensitive.

Parameters
----------
drum_name : str
    Name of a drum which exists in the general MIDI standard.
    If the drum is not found, a ValueError is raised.

Returns
-------
note_number : int
    The MIDI note number corresponding to this drum.

Notes
-----
    See http://www.midi.org/techspecs/gm1sound.php

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.program_to_instrument_name
documentation:
Converts a MIDI program number to the corresponding General MIDI
instrument name.

Parameters
----------
program_number : int
    MIDI program number, between 0 and 127.

Returns
-------
instrument_name : str
    Name of the instrument corresponding to this program number.

Notes
-----
    See http://www.midi.org/techspecs/gm1sound.php

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.instrument_name_to_program
documentation:
Converts an instrument name to the corresponding General MIDI program
number.  Conversion is case, whitespace, and non-alphanumeric character
insensitive.

Parameters
----------
instrument_name : str
    Name of an instrument which exists in the general MIDI standard.
    If the instrument is not found, a ValueError is raised.

Returns
-------
program_number : int
    The MIDI program number corresponding to this instrument.

Notes
-----
    See http://www.midi.org/techspecs/gm1sound.php

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.program_to_instrument_class
documentation:
Converts a MIDI program number to the corresponding General MIDI
instrument class.

Parameters
----------
program_number : int
    MIDI program number, between 0 and 127.

Returns
-------
instrument_class : str
    Name of the instrument class corresponding to this program number.

Notes
-----
    See http://www.midi.org/techspecs/gm1sound.php

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.pitch_bend_to_semitones
documentation:
Convert a MIDI pitch bend value (in the range `[-8192, 8191]`) to the
bend amount in semitones.

# Parameters

pitch_bend

: MIDI pitch bend amount, in `[-8192, 8191]`.

semitone_range

: Convert to +/- this semitone range. Default is 2., which is the
  General MIDI standard +/-2 semitone range.

# Returns

semitones

: Number of semitones corresponding to this pitch bend amount.

functions.name: pretty_midi.utilities.semitones_to_pitch_bend
documentation:
Convert a semitone value to the corresponding MIDI pitch bend integer.

# Parameters

semitones

: Number of semitones for the pitch bend.

semitone_range

: Convert to +/- this semitone range. Default is 2., which is the
  General MIDI standard +/-2 semitone range.

# Returns

pitch_bend

: MIDI pitch bend amount, in `[-8192, 8191]`.

modules.name: pretty_midi.instrument
documentation:
The Instrument class holds all events for a single instrument and contains
functions for extracting information from the events it contains.

classes.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.__init__
documentation: Create the Instrument.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.get_onsets
documentation:
Get all onsets of all notes played by this instrument.
May contain duplicates.

Returns
-------
onsets : np.ndarray
        List of all note onsets.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.get_piano_roll
documentation:
Compute a piano roll matrix of this instrument.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling frequency of the columns, i.e. each column is spaced apart
  by `1./fs` seconds.

times

: Times of the start of each column in the piano roll.
  Default `None` which is `np.arange(0, get_end_time(), 1./fs)`.

pedal_threshold

: Value of control change 64 (sustain pedal) message that is less
  than this value is reflected as pedal-off. Pedals will be
  reflected as elongation of notes in the piano roll.
  If None, then CC64 message is ignored.
  Default is 64.

# Returns

piano_roll

: Piano roll of this instrument.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.get_chroma
documentation:
Get a sequence of chroma vectors from this instrument.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling frequency of the columns, i.e. each column is spaced apart
  by `1./fs` seconds.

times

: Times of the start of each column in the piano roll.
  Default `None` which is `np.arange(0, get_end_time(), 1./fs)`.

pedal_threshold

: Value of control change 64 (sustain pedal) message that is less
  than this value is reflected as pedal-off. Pedals will be
  reflected as elongation of notes in the piano roll.
  If None, then CC64 message is ignored.
  Default is 64.

# Returns

piano_roll

: Chromagram of this instrument.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.get_end_time
documentation:
Returns the time of the end of the events in this instrument.

Returns
-------
end_time : float
    Time, in seconds, of the last event.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.get_pitch_class_histogram
documentation:
Computes the frequency of pitch classes of this instrument,
optionally weighted by their durations or velocities.

Parameters
----------
use_duration : bool
    Weight frequency by note duration.
use_velocity : bool
    Weight frequency by note velocity.
normalize : bool
    Normalizes the histogram such that the sum of bin values is 1.

Returns
-------
histogram : np.ndarray, shape=(12,)
    Histogram of pitch classes given current instrument, optionally
    weighted by their durations or velocities.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.get_pitch_class_transition_matrix
documentation:
Computes the pitch class transition matrix of this instrument.
Transitions are added whenever the end of a note is within
`time_tresh` from the start of any other note.

# Parameters

normalize

: Normalize transition matrix such that matrix sum equals to 1.

time_thresh

: Maximum temporal threshold, in seconds, between the start of a note
  and end time of any other note for a transition to be added.

# Returns

transition_matrix

: Pitch class transition matrix.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.remove_invalid_notes
documentation: Removes any notes whose end time is before or at their start time.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.synthesize
documentation:
Synthesize the instrument's notes using some waveshape.
For drum instruments, returns zeros.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling rate of the synthesized audio signal.

wave

: Function which returns a periodic waveform,
  e.g. `np.sin`, `scipy.signal.square`, etc.

# Returns

synthesized

: Waveform of the instrument's notes, synthesized at `fs`.

methods.name: pretty_midi.instrument.Instrument.fluidsynth
documentation:
Synthesize using fluidsynth.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling rate to synthesize.

sf2_path

: Path to a .sf2 file.
  Default `None`, which uses the TimGM6mb.sf2 file included with
  `pretty_midi`.

# Returns

synthesized

: Waveform of the MIDI data, synthesized at `fs`.

Object to hold event information for a single instrument.

# Parameters

program

: MIDI program number (instrument index), in `[0, 127]`.

is_drum

: Is the instrument a drum instrument (channel 9)?

name

: Name of the instrument.

# Attributes

program

: The program number of this instrument.

is_drum

: Is the instrument a drum instrument (channel 9)?

name

: Name of the instrument.

notes

: List of {class}`pretty_midi.Note` objects.

pitch_bends

: List of of {class}`pretty_midi.PitchBend` objects.

control_changes

: List of {class}`pretty_midi.ControlChange` objects.

modules.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi
documentation:
Utility functions for handling MIDI data in an easy to read/manipulate
format

classes.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.__init__
documentation:
Initialize either by populating it with MIDI data from a file or
from scratch with no data.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI._load_tempo_changes
documentation:
Populates `self._tick_scales` with tuples of
`(tick, tick_scale)` loaded from `midi_data`.

# Parameters

midi_data

: MIDI object from which data will be read.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI._load_metadata
documentation:
Populates `self.time_signature_changes` with `TimeSignature`
objects, `self.key_signature_changes` with `KeySignature` objects,
`self.lyrics` with `Lyric` objects and `self.text_events` with
`Text` objects.

# Parameters

midi_data

: MIDI object from which data will be read.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI._update_tick_to_time
documentation:
Creates `self.__tick_to_time`, a class member array which maps
ticks to time starting from tick 0 and ending at `max_tick`.

# Parameters

max_tick

: Last tick to compute time for. If `self._tick_scales` contains a
  tick which is larger than this value, it will be used instead.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI._load_instruments
documentation:
Populates `self.instruments` using `midi_data`.

# Parameters

midi_data

: MIDI object from which data will be read.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_tempo_changes
documentation:
Return arrays of tempo changes in quarter notes-per-minute and their
times.

# Returns

tempo_change_times

: Times, in seconds, where the tempo changes.

tempi

: What the tempo is, in quarter notes-per-minute, at each point in
  time in `tempo_change_times`.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_end_time
documentation:
Returns the time of the end of the MIDI object (time of the last
event in all instruments/meta-events).

Returns
-------
end_time : float
    Time, in seconds, where this MIDI file ends.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.estimate_tempi
documentation:
Return an empirical estimate of tempos and each tempo's probability.
Based on "Automatic Extraction of Tempo and Beat from Expressive
Performance", Dixon 2001.

Returns
-------
tempos : np.ndarray
    Array of estimated tempos, in beats per minute.
probabilities : np.ndarray
    Array of the probabilities of each tempo estimate.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.estimate_tempo
documentation:
Returns the best tempo estimate from
{func}`pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.estimate_tempi()`, for convenience.

# Returns

tempo

: Estimated tempo, in bpm

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_beats
documentation:
Returns a list of beat locations, according to MIDI tempo changes.
For compound meters (any whose numerator is a multiple of 3 greater
than 3), this method returns every third denominator note (for 6/8
or 6/16 time, for example, it will return every third 8th note or
16th note, respectively). For all other meters, this method returns
every denominator note (every quarter note for 3/4 or 4/4 time, for
example).

Parameters
----------
start_time : float
    Location of the first beat, in seconds.

Returns
-------
beats : np.ndarray
    Beat locations, in seconds.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.estimate_beat_start
documentation:
Estimate the location of the first beat based on which of the first
few onsets results in the best correlation with the onset spike train.

Parameters
----------
candidates : int
    Number of candidate onsets to try.
tolerance : float
    The tolerance in seconds around which onsets will be used to
    treat a beat as correct.

Returns
-------
beat_start : float
    The offset which is chosen as the beat start location.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_downbeats
documentation:
Return a list of downbeat locations, according to MIDI tempo changes
and time signature change events.

Parameters
----------
start_time : float
    Location of the first downbeat, in seconds.

Returns
-------
downbeats : np.ndarray
    Downbeat locations, in seconds.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_onsets
documentation:
Return a sorted list of the times of all onsets of all notes from
all instruments.  May have duplicate entries.

Returns
-------
onsets : np.ndarray
    Onset locations, in seconds.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_piano_roll
documentation:
Compute a piano roll matrix of the MIDI data.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling frequency of the columns, i.e. each column is spaced apart
  by `1./fs` seconds.

times

: Times of the start of each column in the piano roll.
  Default `None` which is `np.arange(0, get_end_time(), 1./fs)`.

pedal_threshold

: Value of control change 64 (sustain pedal) message that is less
  than this value is reflected as pedal-off. Pedals will be
  reflected as elongation of notes in the piano roll.
  If None, then CC64 message is ignored.
  Default is 64.

# Returns

piano_roll

: Piano roll of MIDI data, flattened across instruments.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_pitch_class_histogram
documentation:
Computes the histogram of pitch classes.

Parameters
----------
use_duration : bool
    Weight frequency by note duration.
use_velocity : bool
    Weight frequency by note velocity.
normalize : bool
    Normalizes the histogram such that the sum of bin values is 1.

Returns
-------
histogram : np.ndarray, shape=(12,)
    Histogram of pitch classes given all tracks, optionally weighted
    by their durations or velocities.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_pitch_class_transition_matrix
documentation:
Computes the total pitch class transition matrix of all instruments.
Transitions are added whenever the end of a note is within
`time_thresh` from the start of any other note.

# Parameters

normalize

: Normalize transition matrix such that matrix sum equals is 1.

time_thresh

: Maximum temporal threshold, in seconds, between the start of a note
  and end time of any other note for a transition to be added.

# Returns

pitch_class_transition_matrix

: Pitch class transition matrix.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.get_chroma
documentation:
Get the MIDI data as a sequence of chroma vectors.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling frequency of the columns, i.e. each column is spaced apart
  by `1./fs` seconds.

times

: Times of the start of each column in the piano roll.
  Default `None` which is `np.arange(0, get_end_time(), 1./fs)`.

pedal_threshold

: Value of control change 64 (sustain pedal) message that is less
  than this value is reflected as pedal-off. Pedals will be
  reflected as elongation of notes in the piano roll.
  If None, then CC64 message is ignored.
  Default is 64.

# Returns

piano_roll

: Chromagram of MIDI data, flattened across instruments.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.synthesize
documentation:
Synthesize the pattern using some waveshape. Ignores drum track.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling rate of the synthesized audio signal.

wave

: Function which returns a periodic waveform,
  e.g. `np.sin`, `scipy.signal.square`, etc.

# Returns

synthesized

: Waveform of the MIDI data, synthesized at `fs`.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.fluidsynth
documentation:
Synthesize using fluidsynth.

# Parameters

fs

: Sampling rate to synthesize at.

sf2_path

: Path to a .sf2 file.
  Default `None`, which uses the TimGM6mb.sf2 file included with
  `pretty_midi`.

# Returns

synthesized

: Waveform of the MIDI data, synthesized at `fs`.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.tick_to_time
documentation:
Converts from an absolute tick to time in seconds using
`self.__tick_to_time`.

# Parameters

tick

: Absolute tick to convert.

# Returns

time

: Time in seconds of tick.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.time_to_tick
documentation:
Converts from a time in seconds to absolute tick using
`self._tick_scales`.

# Parameters

time

: Time, in seconds.

# Returns

tick

: Absolute tick corresponding to the supplied time.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.adjust_times
documentation:
Adjusts the timing of the events in the MIDI object.
The parameters `original_times` and `new_times` define a mapping,
so that if an event originally occurs at time `original_times[n]`, it
will be moved so that it occurs at `new_times[n]`. If events don't
occur exactly on a time in `original_times`, their timing will be
linearly interpolated.

# Parameters

original_times

: Times to map from.

new_times

: New times to map to.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.remove_invalid_notes
documentation: Removes any notes whose end time is before or at their start time.

methods.name: pretty_midi.pretty_midi.PrettyMIDI.write
documentation:
Write the MIDI data out to a .mid file.

Parameters
----------
filename : str or file
    Path or file to write .mid file to.

A container for MIDI data in an easily-manipulable format.

# Parameters

midi_file

: Path or file pointer to a MIDI file.
  Default `None` which means create an empty class with the supplied
  values for resolution and initial tempo.

resolution

: Resolution of the MIDI data, when no file is provided.

initial_tempo

: Initial tempo for the MIDI data, when no file is provided.

# Attributes

instruments

: List of {class}`pretty_midi.Instrument` objects.

key_signature_changes

: List of {class}`pretty_midi.KeySignature` objects.

time_signature_changes

: List of {class}`pretty_midi.TimeSignature` objects.

lyrics

: List of {class}`pretty_midi.Lyric` objects.

text_events

: List of {class}`pretty_midi.Text` objects.
